Title: ZmTH1 Is Vital for Healthy Plant Growth and Promotes Cold/Drought Tolerance by Regulating Thiamin Diphosphate-Dependent Metabolisms in Maize

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 (coding sequence for OE construct); mutant identified in M66 × dg166-derived population
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M66 × dg166 F2 for mutant analysis; B104 for knockout/overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: vitamin B1 (thiamine), thiamine monophosphate (TMP), thiamine diphosphate (TDP)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: vitamin B1 (thiamine) biosynthesis and TDP homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxymethylpyrimidine pyrophosphate-related kinase step and thiamine monophosphate synthesis in vitamin B1 biosynthesis as a bifunctional enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: HMP-related pyrimidine intermediate; HET-P and HMP-PP for TMP synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and direct intermediate for vitamin B1/TDP synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to downstream, key bifunctional step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported; essential for VB1 synthesis but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues; detected in roots, stem apexes, leaves at V3/V8/R1, anthers, silks, kernels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues with no significant difference between WT and pldg1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin (Ubi), constitutive, for overexpression and GFP localization constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves were assayed for TMP, VB1 and TDP at V3 stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous VB1 supplementation; cold stress; drought stress; DX treatment in knockout line
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Genome Sequence Archive CRA021719); primary metabolomics (accession not provided); bulk-segregant resequencing (accession not provided)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmTMPS1; TDP-dependent enzymes PDH; α-KGDH; TK; ALS; PDC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; mutant analysis; CRISPR knockout; allelic test; genetic complementation; VB1 rescue; heterologous complementation in E. coli thiD/thie mutants; subcellular localization; LC-MS/MS metabolite quantification; enzyme activity assays; overexpression; stress assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TMP, VB1 and TDP biosynthesis, supports TDP-dependent enzyme activity and normal growth, and enhances cold/drought tolerance when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTH1 was identified as the causal gene of pldg1, and mutation reduced TMP, VB1 and TDP levels. 2. ZmTH1 encodes a chloroplast-localized bifunctional HMPP-K/TMP-S enzyme required for normal plant growth. 3. Loss of ZmTH1 impaired activities of PDH, α-KGDH, TK, ALS and PDC and disrupted multiple TDP-dependent metabolic pathways. 4. Overexpression of ZmTH1 increased TMP and VB1 accumulation, enhanced TDP-dependent enzyme activities and improved seedling cold and drought tolerance. 5. VB1 supplementation rescued mutant defects, confirming its role in VB1 metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout caused albinism and lethality; overexpression increased TMP and slightly VB1 but decreased measured TDP level, likely reflecting homeostatic adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering vitamin B1 metabolism and improving maize cold/drought resilience; can be used in overexpression or metabolic engineering strategies, potentially combined with VB1 fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The pldg1 allele retains HMPP-K but loses TMP-S activity due to frameshift truncation lacking TMP-S domain; knockout lacking both domains is lethal. DX partially alleviated ko albino phenotype, linking ZmTH1 function to MEP-pathway-dependent pigment synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73/maize germplasm used in this study
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M66 × dg166 F2 for expression analysis; maize protoplasts; tobacco for transient localization
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TMP-S domain only; 94.6% identity within TMP-S domain to ZmTH1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes TMP synthesis with limited efficiency, partially compensating for loss of ZmTH1 TMP-S activity.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate for vitamin B1 synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in root, stem and leaves of WT at V3/V8; expressed in all tissues after R1 stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced significantly in pldg1 root, stem and leaf tissues at early stages; similar to ZmTH1 after R1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin (Ubi), constitutive, in maize protoplasts; 35S, constitutive, in tobacco transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Functional compensation observed under ZmTH1 mutation background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic/domain analysis; heterologous complementation in E. coli thie mutant; qRT-PCR expression profiling; subcellular localization in maize protoplast and tobacco leaf
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides limited TMP-S activity that partially compensates for ZmTH1 deficiency but cannot fully restore VB1 levels or normal growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTMPS1 is a paralog of ZmTH1 encoding only a TMP-S domain. 2. It complements E. coli thie but not thid, confirming TMP-S-only activity. 3. Its catalytic efficiency is lower than ZmTH1. 4. ZmTMPS1 is induced early in pldg1 and partially compensates for ZmTH1 TMP-S loss. 5. It localizes to the cytosol and nucleus, expanding the compartmentation of maize VB1 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compensation is insufficient to fully rescue ZmTH1 deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for strengthening VB1 biosynthesis, though lower activity suggests limited standalone utility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer feedback regulation by ZmTH1 because ZmTMPS1 expression is induced earlier when ZmTH1 loses TMP-S function.
